original_hypothesis.title: Admin Can Drain Allocated Reward Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The admin can use the `recover` function in `LockingMultiRewards` to withdraw all reward tokens, including those already allocated to users, due to missing balance checks.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: In `LockingMultiRewards.sol`, the `recover` function includes a safeguard to prevent skimming more than the excess balance of the `stakingToken`. However, it lacks any such check for the `rewardTokens`. A malicious or compromised admin can call `recover(rewardToken, balance)` to drain the entire reward pool, leaving users unable to claim their earned and locked rewards.

proof_of_signal.pattern: recover lacks balance checks for reward tokens

preconditions: Admin key is compromised or admin acts maliciously
Reward tokens have been funded to the contract

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No check against `_rewardData[token].rewardRate` or user balances
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin calls `recover` with the full balance of a reward token
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker steals 100% of the reward pool.

economic_questions: Is the admin a multisig or a single EOA?
Are reward tokens funded in large batches?
validation_requirements: Simulate `recover` on an active reward token
Verify that subsequent user claims revert due to insufficient balance

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of user rewards and trust

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of all pending and locked rewards
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total balance of all reward tokens

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
